model_narrative: We run a production and distribution network where several factories make a product and ship it to distribution centers that serve uncertain market demand. Demand is captured by a set of scenarios, each with its own probability. Before demand is known we decide how much each factory produces and how much it ships to each distribution center, which fixes how much arrives at each center. Once a scenario's demand is revealed, we decide how much of the arriving product is sold and how much goes unsold as waste. The objective is to maximize the expected profit, where profit credits sales revenue, charges for unsold waste, and subtracts the transportation and production costs, with the sales-and-waste part averaged over the scenarios by their probabilities.

sets.name: I
sets.doc: the factories that produce and ship product

sets.name: J
sets.doc: the distribution centers, also called markets, that receive product and serve demand

sets.name: S
sets.doc: the demand scenarios, each a possible realization of market demand with its own probability

params.name: capacity
params.doc: the maximum quantity each factory can produce, in units

params.name: prodcost
params.doc: the production cost per unit produced at any factory, in dollars per unit

params.name: price
params.doc: the sales price earned per unit sold at any market, in dollars per unit

params.name: wastecost
params.doc: the cost per unit of product that is received at a market but not sold, in dollars per unit

params.name: transcost
params.doc: the transportation cost per unit shipped from a factory to a distribution center, in dollars per unit

params.name: scen_prob
params.doc: the probability of each demand scenario; the probabilities across scenarios sum to one

params.name: scen_demand
params.doc: the demand at each distribution center under each scenario, in units

vars.name: ship
vars.doc: the quantity shipped from each factory to each distribution center

vars.name: product
vars.doc: the quantity produced at each factory

vars.name: received
vars.doc: the quantity that arrives at each distribution center; this is decided before the demand scenario is known and is the same across all scenarios

vars.name: sales
vars.doc: the quantity sold at each distribution center under each scenario

vars.name: waste
vars.doc: the quantity received at each distribution center that goes unsold under each scenario, treated as overstock

description: Each factory's output must match everything it ships out. For each factory, the quantity it produces equals the total quantity it ships across all the distribution centers.

description: Whatever arrives at a distribution center is either sold or left unsold, in every scenario. For each distribution center under each scenario, the quantity sold plus the quantity wasted equals the quantity that arrived at that center.
